criteria.label: Execute the PCA workflow: center data, fit PCA, and project to k components with sklearn.
criteria.example: On Iris, center X, fit PCA(n_components=2), print explained_variance_ratio_ and Z.shape == (150, 2).

criteria.label: Interpret explained variance ratio and pick k using a cumulative threshold.
criteria.example: Report the smallest k where cumulative explained variance ≥ 0.90 for a 20-feature sensor matrix.

criteria.label: Relate PCA directions to covariance eigenvectors and SVD of centered X without re-deriving spectral theory.
criteria.example: Show that np.linalg.svd(X_centered)[2] rows align with sklearn PCA components_ up to sign and that σᵢ²/(n−1) ranks variance.

criteria.label: Choose appropriately between covariance PCA and correlation PCA for mixed-scale features.
criteria.example: Given age and income columns, explain why StandardScaler before PCA changes the first component loadings compared to raw covariance PCA.

criteria.label: Fit PCA without data leakage in a train/test or cross-validation pipeline.
criteria.example: Build Pipeline([StandardScaler(), PCA(5), LogisticRegression()]) and fit only on X_train; transform X_test with the fitted pipeline.
